title: rubus × hypomacrophyllus
intro: Rubus × hypomacrophyllus, also known as the hybrid raspberry, is a cross between the European raspberry (Rubus idaeus) and the Himalayan raspberry (Rubus nepalensis). It is a thorny, deciduous shrub native to the Himalayan region, valued for its medicinal properties and health benefits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments due to its high content of anthocyanins, ellagic acid, and other antioxidants, which have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and anti-cancer properties. Rubus × hypomacrophyllus can be prepared as herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties, and is often used to support cardiovascular health, boost the immune system, and protect against oxidative stress.
